findings.title: SQL Injection in Login Form
findings.description: The username parameter of the login endpoint is concatenated directly into a SQL query. A boolean-based blind injection allowed authentication bypass and enumeration of the users table.
findings.remediation: Use parameterised queries / prepared statements for all database access. Apply input validation and least-privilege database accounts.

findings.title: Stored Cross-Site Scripting in Profile Bio
findings.description: The profile bio field stores and renders unsanitised HTML, allowing JavaScript execution in the context of any user viewing the profile.
findings.remediation: Context-aware output encoding on render; apply a strict Content-Security-Policy.

findings.title: JWT Signature Not Verified (alg:none Accepted)
findings.description: The API accepts JWTs signed with the 'none' algorithm, allowing an attacker to forge tokens with arbitrary claims including role=admin.
findings.remediation: Enforce a fixed signing algorithm server-side and reject 'none'. Validate signatures on every request.

findings.title: Missing HTTP Security Headers
findings.description: Responses lack Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, and Strict-Transport-Security headers.
findings.remediation: Add CSP, X-Frame-Options: DENY, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, and HSTS with max-age >= 31536000.

findings.title: Verbose Server Version Disclosure
findings.description: The Server header discloses the exact web server and framework versions, aiding targeted attacks.
findings.remediation: Suppress or genericise version banners in server and framework configuration.
